title: backup script: stash branch, switch to main to commit, restore branch
description: backup-home.sh skips git commit/push when not on main. Fix: save current branch, stash WIP, switch to main, pull, commit+push snapshot, switch back and pop stash.

title: Follow up Ubuntu silent reset and OpenClaw post-boot service failures
description: Latest crash on 2026-04-28 remains a silent-reset class with no panic/OOM trail; preserved evidence points to a hard reset plus post-boot OpenClaw startup-check PATH failure and qdrant Docker-readiness failure. Follow up on hardware/display/session hypotheses and service wiring.

title: Investigate recent crash evidence
Add scripts/diagnose_ui_freeze.sh to user_scope repo
description: One-command diagnostic for the failure mode hit on 2026-04-25. Should print: swapon --show + zero-swap warn; top 5 services by 'restart counter' in current boot; container churn rate (docker events last 1h); /proc/pressure/{cpu,memory,io} snapshot; journalctl -p err -b -1 line count; abrupt-boot detection via boot-boundary timestamp gap + missing 'Stopping'/'Reached target shutdown' markers. Repo currently has no display/UI/GPU diagnostic scripts.

title: Kernel hypothesis falsified by 6.8 LTS panic-class match (2026-05-01)
description: 6.8.0-110 LTS panicked in 25 min with identical __update_blocked_fair signature as 6.17. Single-variable kernel swap exonerates kernel; cause is in shared lower layer (modules/hardware/microcode).

title: If 6.8+595 still crashes: Memtest86+ overnight (in GRUB menu) → 12VHPWR/PSU/RAM hardware path
description: Conditional. Memtest first (4+ passes, 6-8h). Then visual 12VHPWR inspection, PSU swap, CPU pins.

title: Isolate which variable mattered: GSP-disable revert, then driver revert, then kernel revert (3 sub-tests over weeks)
description: Isolation order after the current soak: if 6.17.0-22 + 595.58.03 + GSP-off is stable, next reboot into 6.8 with the same driver/cmdline to isolate the kernel variable. If 6.8 is also stable, later test GSP-on and older driver only if worth the churn. If the current soak crashes, 6.8 becomes the immediate recovery/isolation step.

title: Re-queue Memtest86+ overnight (4+ passes, 6-8h) — required to rule out DDR5
description: Pre-flight verified clean (Secure Boot off, binary v7.00 present, EFI partition mounted, mechanism works). Need uninterrupted run. Pre-flight command: `sudo grub-reboot 'memtest86+'`. Photo screen if errors appear (no disk logging from memtest).

title: Test pre-2026-04-14 intel-microcode (co-confounder with kernel HWE; only kernel was rolled back)
description: intel-microcode bumped on 2026-04-14 unattended-upgrade alongside kernel HWE. Could shift IMC timings. Untested. Roll back via apt: `sudo apt install intel-microcode=<old-version>`. Source older version from launchpad if not in apt cache.

title: Memtest86+ via grub-reboot one-shot reliable; user must leave box for 6-8h uninterrupted
description: Verified mechanism works (next_entry consumed). Last attempt 2026-05-01 only got 12 min before user-side interrupt. Re-queue with `sudo grub-reboot 'memtest86+'` BEFORE long away period.

title: Validate power-state-varying metrics under load before declaring anomaly
description: PCIe link speed misdiagnosed on RTX 4090; idle reads 2.5GT/s but reaches 16GT/s under CUDA load. Generalize: any metric varied by power state must be re-checked under load.

title: Add memtest queue helper script
Deferred 6.8 LTS verification if current 6.17+595+GSP-off soak fails or stabilizes
description: Use ~/post-reboot-checklist.md only after either (a) current 6.17.0-22 + 595.58.03 + GSP-off boot crashes, requiring recovery on 6.8, or (b) current soak stabilizes and we intentionally reboot into 6.8 to isolate the kernel variable.

title: fix Dropbox rsync: EDEADLK on metadata write + unexpected EOF on large files
description: Two failures: (1) cat > home_backup_metadata.txt hits EDEADLK because Dropbox locks the file — fix with tmp+mv. (2) rsync unexpected EOF on large openclaw files — fix with --inplace flag.

title: Re-register or permanently retire org-runner-* containers (proximate cause of 2026-04-25 UI freeze)
description: All 10 myoung34/github-runner containers were stopped + restart=no on 2026-04-25 to halt the registration-deleted respawn storm. Decide: re-register with fresh PAT (Classic, workflow scope) per setup-org-runners skill, OR retire them. Container restart policy must NOT be 'unless-stopped' — wrap launch in launchd/systemd that handles auth-fail cleanly. See ref memory: reference_2026-04-25_github_runner_storm.md
close_reason: soft-start.sh + jleechanorg-org-runners systemd; use start-runner for reprovision

title: Portable self-hosted runner install: drop .env-on-host pattern, single sudo ./install.sh on fresh clone
description: Goal: git clone jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai && sudo ./install.sh sets up org runners on fresh Linux box. Today's outage (2026-04-26) exposed brittleness of soft-start.sh + start-runner.sh sourcing a hand-edited working-tree .env (PAT lives there too). Handoff doc: ~/roadmap/2026-04-26-portable-self-hosted-runners-handoff.md. Land PR #6681 first (independent freeze fix). Do NOT incrementally migrate config — go straight to /etc/jleechanorg-org-runners/{config.env,secrets.env mode 0600} and bake identity into Docker containers at run time. Multi-host coexistence required (mikey ARM64 host on a different machine).

title: Use /secondo when convergent evidence feels strong (confirmation-bias prophylactic)
description: Adversarial second opinion (Gemini) caught wrong PCIe-degradation diagnosis reinforced by matching public bug. Invoke /secondo BEFORE publishing high-stakes diagnoses.

title: Silent-stop hard reset diagnostic recipe (no panic, no pstore)
description: Recipe for Linux hard-resets with empty pstore + silent kernel ring: hardware until proven otherwise. Check pstore, NVMe unsafe_shutdowns, PCIe link (under load) before software theories.

title: Mask snap.snapd-desktop-integration restart loop on Jeff-Ubuntu
description: User must run `systemctl --user mask --now snap.snapd-desktop-integration.snapd-desktop-integration.service` from a graphical login session. Verified 53 restart counter events in 2 minutes still ongoing as of 2026-04-25 14:00. Known upstream bug: canonical/snapd-desktop-integration#71

title: Jeff-Ubuntu 48h soak test on 6.8 LTS + nvidia 595 + GSP-disabled
description: FAILED: 6.8 LTS panicked in 25 min (2026-04-30 23:43:19) with identical signature class as 6.17. Kernel hypothesis falsified.

title: Wire disk_snapshot snapshot_warning to Slack alert
description: disk_snapshot.sh now emits snapshot_warning='low_coverage' when sum(directories)/disk_used_kb < 70%. Create a post-snapshot validator script that reads backup/Mac/disk_snapshot.json and posts to Slack if warning != null. Add it to the cron schedule that runs disk_snapshot.sh (currently inside backup-home.sh). Reference: scripts/disk_snapshot.sh; test in tests/test_disk_snapshot.py.

title: Tier-2 observability: enable full sysrq, hardware watchdog, kdump, PSI watcher
description: Post-freeze hardening on Jeff-Ubuntu. (1) `kernel.sysrq=1` via sysctl drop-in; (2) modprobe iTCO_wdt + apt install watchdog with /etc/watchdog.conf max-load-1=24 + PSI test; (3) apt install linux-crashdump (kdump); (4) systemd unit watching /proc/pressure/memory and alerting when full avg10>30. Goal: next freeze is diagnosable from in-tree state, not requiring power-cycle.
close_reason: install-ui-freeze-observability.sh: sysrq=1, iTCO load, watchdog, kdump, psi-memory-watcher.service

title: Wire disk_snapshot.sh --discover to weekly cron
description: Catches NEW large dirs that aren't yet in MONITORED_DIRS (the original failure class: .gemini 138G and .ao-sessions 62G were silently absent). Add a weekly launchd job that runs 'scripts/disk_snapshot.sh --discover' and posts any UNTRACKED >5GB dirs to Slack.

Add launchd cleanup for ~/projects/worktree_* older than 21 days
description: Currently 83 worktrees in ~/projects totaling 44.7 GB; 18 are >14 days old. AO and other automation creates worktrees per task with no rotation. Build launchd plist + cleanup script that archives or deletes worktree_* dirs older than 21 days with clean git status. Mtime <14d remains protected per repo CLAUDE.md (WORKTREE APPROVED rule). Reclaim potential: ~7-15 GB recurring.
